snippet.title: Micron's $825 million investment to boost semiconductor industry: CEO Sanjay Mehrotra
snippet.description:
In a significant boost to the semiconductor industry, Micron has announced plans to invest $825 million across two phases. This investment, combined with financial contributions from Central and state governments, could see a total outlay of up to $2.75 billion. Sanjay Mehrotra, President and CEO of Micron, stated that this support from the government, which includes facilitating access to crucial semiconductor resources, will stimulate innovation and foster the development of local talent.

snippet.title: What are the Best Ways to Lead a Healthy Life? | Dr Sanjay Mehrotra
snippet.description:
Living a healthy life is not challenging if you have a strong determination to resist excess junk food, alcohol, smoking, and tobacco! You can positively shape your lifestyle by doing what is best for your health. Dr Sanjay Mehrotra, Senior Consultant-Cardiology at Narayana Health City, Bangalore, discusses some efficient methods to live a healthy, happy, stress-free, and enjoyable life! 

On the grand occasion of the 75th anniversary of World Health Day, the doctor wishes everyone a healthy heart and a healthy life!

Eating meals timely is important for the health.
Regular exercising is important for the good functioning of the heart .
Eating 3 meals a day is important to fuel the body with the energy it needs to function.
Eating good amount of fresh fruits and green leafy vegetables for improved digestion. 
Consuming more protein for body maintenance. 

Maintain your body's fitness and health by following an everyday schedule of physical activities such as yoga, running, walking, swimming, and other sports of your choice! 

#TakeCare of your mind by avoiding unnecessary anxiety and stress of modern-day lifestyle. Focus on meditation to improve your mental health!

snippet.title: Sanjay Mehrotra, CEO, Micron Technology at Semicond India | DT Next
snippet.description:
#microntechnology #sanjaymehrotra #pmmodi 
Sanjay Mehrotra, CEO, Micron Technology at Semicond India | DT Next

Sanjay Mehrotra, CEO, Micron Technology at Semicond India, says, "I thank PM Modi for the vision to make India a global hub for semiconductors...Micron is committed to building a semiconductor assembly and test facility in Gujarat. We estimate that our project in Gujarat will create nearly 5000 direct jobs and additional 15,000 jobs in the community. We are hopeful that this investment will help catalyse other investments in the sector...Digital India and Make in India are creating a truly transformative energy that will continue to drive positive progress..."

snippet.title: Visionary Encounters: PM Modi's Impact on Micron CEO - Unleashing India's Potential #shorts
snippet.description:
Visionary Encounters: PM Modi's Impact on Micron CEO - Unleashing India's Potential #shorts @MeditatingMindz 

In this video, we delve into the extraordinary meeting between Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi and Sanjay Mehrotra, the CEO of Micron Technology. Join us as we explore the profound impact of PM Modi's vision for India, which leaves the esteemed CEO deeply impressed. Witness the convergence of visionary leadership and technological potential as PM Modi sets the stage for India's remarkable journey towards global excellence.

This video takes you behind the scenes of a historic encounter that unveils the power of vision and collaboration. Experience the shared passion for progress as PM Modi engages in a thought-provoking conversation with Sanjay Mehrotra, an accomplished leader in the semiconductor industry and the CEO of Micron Technology, a renowned global semiconductor company.

As the video unfolds, we delve into the key highlights of PM Modi's meeting with Sanjay Mehrotra. The CEO's deep admiration for PM Modi's vision for India's transformation sets the tone for an engaging discussion. They explore the immense potential of India's technology landscape, discussing strategic collaborations, investments, and the role of innovation in shaping the country's future.

Furthermore, the video examines PM Modi's commitment to fostering technological advancements and creating an environment conducive to innovation. It highlights the government's initiatives aimed at promoting research and development, nurturing talent, and bolstering the digital infrastructure. PM Modi's vision for a technologically empowered India resonates strongly with Sanjay Mehrotra, igniting a powerful sense of possibility and collaboration.

The video delves into the broader implications of this visionary encounter. It explores the potential for transformative partnerships, job creation, and economic growth. Experts and industry insiders provide insights into the significance of PM Modi's vision and its alignment with the goals of companies like Micron Technology. They discuss how this meeting can serve as a catalyst for the expansion of India's semiconductor and technology sectors, positioning the nation as a global leader.

Moreover, the video sheds light on the collaborative spirit between PM Modi and Sanjay Mehrotra. It highlights the CEO's deep appreciation for India's skilled workforce, entrepreneurial spirit, and the favorable investment climate. PM Modi's emphasis on building robust infrastructure and nurturing a vibrant startup ecosystem resonates strongly with Sanjay Mehrotra, fostering a sense of optimism and a shared commitment to realizing India's potential.

As the video nears its conclusion, we reflect on the transformative power of visionary leadership. Witness the fusion of PM Modi's bold vision and Sanjay Mehrotra's deep industry expertise, driving India towards technological excellence. Prepare to be inspired, informed, and intrigued as we unravel the profound impact of PM Modi's meeting with Micron CEO Sanjay Mehrotra in "Visionary Encounters: PM Modi's Impact on Micron CEO - Unleashing India's Potential.

snippet.title: Why Value Investors Love Micron Technology So Much...
snippet.description:
Micron technology had been a famous stock holding in many well know value investors such as Mohnish Pabrai and LiLu. But why is this the case, Over the past decade, Micron Technology's stock has experienced substantial growth and fluctuations. However, starting from late 2018, Micron's stock faced a downturn due to various factors such as oversupply concerns, trade tensions, and weakening demand. The stock price experienced significant volatility during this period, with fluctuations reflecting market sentiment and industry trends.
With all of those headwinds, why does micron technology is so loved by value investors. So, in today’s video we are going to take a look at the memory giant Micron Technology. snippet.title: Top Indian CEO's who did Masters in the USA
snippet.description:
In this video, we delve into the inspiring journeys of five remarkable Indian CEOs who pursued their Masters in the United States. Join us as we explore the incredible accomplishments of Sundar Pichai, Satya Nadella, Shantanu Narayen, Arvind Krishna, and Sanjay Mehrotra.

These visionary leaders have not only left an indelible mark on the global business landscape but have also become iconic figures for aspiring entrepreneurs and professionals around the world. Through their sheer determination, intellect, and relentless pursuit of excellence, they have transformed the tech industry and continue to shape the future of innovation.

Discover how Sundar Pichai, the CEO of Alphabet Inc. and its subsidiary Google, navigated his way from the Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) to the prestigious Stanford University.

Witness how Satya Nadella, the CEO of Microsoft, revolutionized the company and spearheaded its transformation into a cloud computing powerhouse after his studies at the University of Wisconsin and the University of Chicago.

Delve into the story of Shantanu Narayen, the CEO of Adobe Inc., who harnessed his education at the University of California, Berkeley to lead the company's creative software revolution. 

Uncover the remarkable journey of Arvind Krishna, the CEO of IBM, who leveraged his education at the University of Illinois at Urbana-Champaign to guide one of the world's most influential technology companies.

Finally, explore the ascent of Sanjay Mehrotra, the CEO of Micron Technology, and witness how his education at Stanford University played a pivotal role in his transformative leadership in the semiconductor industry.

snippet.title: इस अमेरिकन कंपनी के CEO को पसंद हैं खान मार्केट के चाट, गोलगप्‍पे
snippet.description:
आज हम आपको बताने जा रहे हैं एक ऐसे भारतवंशी की कहानी जो अब भारत के सेमीकंडक्‍टर मिशन से जुड़ने जा रहा है. कानपुर के रहने वाले इस शख्‍स का नाम है संजय मेहरोत्रा. उत्‍तर प्रदेश के रहने वाले संजय मेहरोत्रा का जन्‍म कानपुर में हुआ है. संजय की शुरुआती पढ़ाई दिल्‍ली के सरदार पटेल स्‍कूल में हुई है. मीडिया रिपोर्ट के अनुसार संजय मल्‍होत्रा ने एक इंटरव्‍यू के दौरान कहा था कि कैसे अपने दोस्‍तों के साथ जाकर खान मार्केट में गोलगप्‍पे, और चाट खाया करते थे. उन्‍हें दिल्‍ली के भल्‍ले पापड़ी, आलू चाट, और पापड़ी चाट बहुत पसंद हैं. संजय की कंपनी गुजरात में चिप बनाने का प्‍लांट लगाने जा रही है.
Today we are going to tell you the story of such an Indian who will join India's semiconductor mission. The name of this person is  Sanjay Mehrotra.  Sanjay Mehrotra, a resident of Uttar Pradesh, was born in Kanpur. Sanjay did his early education at Sardar Patel School in Delhi. According to media reports,  Sanjay Mehrotra said during an interview that Khan used to eat Golgappas and chaat in the market with his friends. He likes Delhi's Bhalle Papdi, Aloo Chaat, and Papdi Chaat very much. Sanjay's company is going to set up a chip manufacturing plant in Gujarat.

snippet.title: IN CONVERSATION- J N KAUL
snippet.description:
Jagan Nath Kaul was the founder and patron of SOS Children's Village of India, providing homes for thousands of children across the country. His dedication to child welfare earned him numerous awards and honors, including the Padma Shri, and he played a pivotal role in establishing SOS Children's Villages in India.

Rajiv Mehrotra has been a pioneering Television anchor & reporter in India. Over several decades from the early1970s into the 2000’s, he was host to various programmes on India’s public service television channel – Doordarshan. This included a series of in-depth Conversations in many avatars that featured the best Indian minds and several global personalities. It was the most watched programme of its genre. Many of them are now available online.

His documentary films won more than three hundred global awards, including thirty-three since 1986 from five Indian Presidents. His books have been published globally in 50+ languages and editions. He was elected a Judge of the Templeton Prize for Scientific & Spiritual Curiosity and a Global Leader for Tomorrow by the World Economic Forum at Davos. Rajiv was educated at the Universities of Delhi, Oxford & Columbia.

Today Rajiv serves as the Secretary & Trustee of the Foundation for Universal Responsibility of HH The Dalai Lama, established with the Nobel Peace Prize. He has been a student of His Holiness for more than forty years.

File Information: Archival Interview

snippet.title: Micron’s investment in India will create 80,000 jobs: Rajeev Chandrasekhar
snippet.description:
Union Minister Rajeev Chandrasekhar, while talking to ANI asserted that after the announcement of US company Micron’s investment in India, employment opportunities will be increased. He asserted that this announcement will bring in a minimum of 80,000 direct jobs. “The three announcements that were made yesterday, a minimum of 80,000 jobs will be there as per me. What will be the indirect consequence will be known later with time, I believe most of the job creation will happen in this sector and will continue to happen…”

snippet.title: Drew Barrymore apologizes to writers
snippet.description: Drew Barrymore is apologizing to television and film writers over her decision to resume production on her talk show as members of the Writers Guild of America remain on strike. Barrymore’s show drew protest from the union after announcing they would resume production without WGA writers contributing. It is not the only talk show that has remained on air or resumed production amid the ongoing writers’ and actors’ strikes. SAG-AFTRA has said Barrymore is in compliance with her contract as host of the program and the production is not struck. Hours after posting her apology video, Barrymore seemingly removed it from her Instagram account. #cnn #news

snippet.title: Micron Tech To Invest $875M In Gujrat Semiconductor Testing Plant | CNBC TV18
snippet.description:
U.S. memory chip firm Micron Technology, Inc (MU.O) said on Thursday it would invest up to $825 million in a new chip assembly and test facility in Gujarat, India, its first factory in the country.

Micron said that with support from the Indian central government and from the state of Gujarat, the total investment in the facility will be $2.75 billion. Of that total, 50% will come from the Indian central government and 20% from the state of Gujarat .

Micron said that with support from the Indian central government and from the state of Gujarat, the total investment in the facility will be $2.75 billion. Of that total, 50% will come from the Indian central government and 20% from the state of Gujarat .

snippet.title: TiEcon 2023 Grand Keynote: Manish Bhatia, Micron, and Mark Patel,  McKinsey
snippet.description:
TiEcon 2023 at Santa Clara Convention Center, May 3-5

Day 3 Grand Keynote: Manish Bhatia, Executive Vice President of Global Operations at
Micron Technology, Inc, in Conversation with Mark Patel, Senior Partner at McKinsey and Company.

Manish Bhatia is the executive vice president of Global Operations at Micron Technology. He is responsible for the companys end-to-end operations delivering industry leading technology and products at scale. His scope includes Fab and Assembly/Test manufacturing, Packaging and Test development, Quality, Supply Chain, Procurement as well as IT, Smart Manufacturing and Artificial Intelligence initiatives. Mr. Bhatia joined Micron in 2017. Previously, Mr. Bhatia served as the Executive Vice President of Silicon Operations at Western Digital Corporation. Prior to that, Mr. Bhatia held several executive roles at SanDisk Corporation and was the companys executive Vice President of Worldwide Operations when it was acquired by Western Digital. Prior to SanDisk, Mr. Bhatia held various positions at Matrix Semiconductor and in consulting at McKinsey and Company and Saint Gobain Corporation. Mr. Bhatia earned bachelors and masters degrees in mechanical engineering from the Massachusetts Institute of Technology and a masters degree in business administration from MIT Sloan School of Management, which he attended as a Leaders for Manufacturing fellow.


Mark Patel is a Senior Partner at McKinsey and Company in the San Francisco Office. Mark focuses on serving clients where technologies come together in hard and complex ways, for maximum global impact. He co-leads the McKinsey Platform for Climate Technologies (MPCT), founded and leads McKinsey Internet of Things (IoT) work globally and is a leader in our global Semiconductor Practice. Mark is also a member of McKinsey own Technology Council. Mark engages with clients across sectors on strategy and operational topics. He has experience leading a diverse of engagements, from high growth programs to turnarounds. In all cases, he works closely with management to define a plan and deliver impact. A passionate believer in the impact that disruptive technologies (e.g., IoT, AI, next gen computing, synthetic biology) will have on improving our lives, work, climate, society, and health, Mark has committed his client and professional focus to these issues. Mark has led programs to support some of the most iconic companies in advanced industries to embrace disruptive technologies across their products and operations. He combines an understanding of technology with has deep operational experience. His recent work has also included supporting clients in their development of resilient global supply chains and navigation of future manufacturing strategy. As a leader within McKinsey global Sustainability work, Mark set up and co-leads our Platform for Climate Technologies (MPCT). Within the MPCT, 12 global priority technologies have been identified and a team of over 500 colleagues globally are now engaged to directly support clients in the development and scaling of the most important technologies for the net zero transition. Mark is also a champion for decarbonization in key sectors. He co-leads McKinsey work to support decarbonization in semiconductors, an effort which is close to his heart and experience. Mark has hands on experience in building early-stage high growth businesses. Outside McKinsey, he was part of the management team that grew a synthetic biology and renewables business from lab to commercial scale and IPO in less than 5 years and where he continued as public company executive officer. Previously Mark was part of a team which created one of Europe’s first mobile application software businesses, building on novel NLP technology. Mark started his career as an automotive engineer with Ford in the UK. Mark holds an MBA from Stanford University and both Masters and Bachelors degrees in Manufacturing Engineering from the University of Cambridge, UK.
